narrative:
Former President Bill Clinton testified under oath to the House Oversight Committee last week regarding his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, with video of the depositions released Monday [1]. The testimony spanned over two days and addressed Clinton's knowledge of Epstein's crimes and the nature of their association [1].

## Key Testimony Points

Clinton testified that he had no knowledge of Epstein's sex trafficking activities, stating "There's nothing that I saw when I was around him that made me realize he was trafficking women" [1]. He told lawmakers he first met Epstein in 2002 when he boarded the financier's private jet for humanitarian work, but their relationship ended a year later in 2003 [1][2].

Clinton acknowledged taking "at least 26 times" on Epstein's aircraft, many as part of his global AIDS initiative [2]. He testified that his final flight on what has been dubbed the "Lolita Express" occurred in 2003, five years before Epstein's 2008 guilty plea [2].

## Hot Tub Photo Questioning

During the four-and-a-half-hour deposition, Clinton was shown a photo from the Justice Department's Epstein files showing him in a hot tub with an unidentified woman whose face was redacted [2]. Clinton testified the photo was taken in Brunei and that he did not remember who the woman was, stating "I had forgotten that there was anybody in the hot tub" [2]. When asked directly if he engaged in sexual activity with the woman, Clinton responded "No" [2].

Clinton described the setting as a hotel pool area next to suites where he and his team were staying on invitation from the sultan of Brunei, with a Secret Service agent present "at the other end of the pool" [2]. He testified that no minors were present in the pool area [2].

## Trump Comments

Clinton also recounted a conversation with Donald Trump in which Trump allegedly told him about having "some great times" with Epstein [1]. According to Clinton's testimony, Trump said "You know, we had some great times together over the years, but we fell out all because of a real estate deal" [1]. Clinton added that Trump "never said anything to me to make me think he was involved in anything improper with regard to Epstein" [1].

## Hillary Clinton's Testimony

Hillary Clinton provided her own deposition a day earlier, telling the committee she does not remember meeting Epstein [1]. Neither Clinton has been accused of wrongdoing in connection with Epstein [1].
